title: A song my taxi driver played in Mumbai
summary: A short story about a song that shaped a single car ride and stayed with you for years.
topic: A song my taxi driver played in Mumbai
prompt: Write 8–10 sentences (150–250 words) about a song or piece of music you heard for the first time during a taxi or bus ride abroad. Describe the moment and why it stayed with you. Use past simple and past continuous.
hintStarters: On my last evening in…, I took a taxi from … to…
While we were driving along…, the driver turned up the radio…
I did not understand a word, but…

hintVocab.meaningVi: in vào đầu, không quên được

sampleText: On my last evening in Mumbai, I took a black-and-yellow taxi from a small bookshop near Colaba back to my hotel. The driver was a thin young man with bright eyes and a small wooden bird on his dashboard. We got stuck in heavy traffic almost immediately because of an early monsoon shower. The horns of other cars were loud all around us, but the driver did not look angry. He smiled, turned up the radio, and said, 'You like this song? My favourite.' A man with a warm low voice started to sing in Hindi over a slow rhythm and a single string instrument. I did not understand a word, but the song was full of something I recognised. It felt like one of those evenings when you remember someone you do not see anymore. While we were waiting at the same red light for the third time, the driver hummed along and tapped the steering wheel with two fingers. When we finally arrived at my hotel, I asked him for the name of the song. He wrote it on a piece of paper torn from a notebook. I still listen to it on quiet evenings, even now, four years later. The song is stuck in my head, and so is the small wooden bird.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Past continuous + past simple for a scene
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Past continuous tả việc đang diễn ra trong nền ('we were waiting at the same red light'), past simple cho hành động chen vào ('the driver hummed along and tapped the steering wheel'). Hai thì thường đi cùng trong kể chuyện.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: Past continuous sets the ongoing background ('we were waiting at the same red light'); past simple marks the events inside the scene ('the driver hummed along and tapped the steering wheel'). The two tenses commonly mix in storytelling.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: 'Get stuck + in/at + noun' idiom
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'Get stuck in (traffic/snow/the rain)' = bị kẹt trong: 'We got stuck in heavy traffic.' Đây là một collocation cố định ở A2.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'Get stuck in (traffic / snow / the rain)' is a fixed A2 collocation: 'We got stuck in heavy traffic.'

sampleTranslationVi: Vào tối cuối cùng của tôi ở Mumbai, tôi đi một chiếc taxi đen-vàng từ một hiệu sách nhỏ gần Colaba về khách sạn. Tài xế là một thanh niên gầy với đôi mắt sáng và một con chim gỗ nhỏ trên bảng điều khiển xe. Chúng tôi bị kẹt trong tắc đường nặng gần như ngay lập tức vì một cơn mưa rào đầu mùa. Tiếng còi của các xe khác vang lên khắp xung quanh, nhưng tài xế không tỏ ra giận dữ. Anh ấy mỉm cười, vặn to đài lên, và nói, 'Bạn thích bài này không? Bài yêu thích của tôi.' Một người đàn ông với giọng hát ấm và trầm bắt đầu hát bằng tiếng Hindi trên một nhịp điệu chậm và một nhạc cụ dây duy nhất. Tôi không hiểu một từ nào, nhưng bài hát đầy ắp một điều gì đó tôi nhận ra. Nó giống như một trong những buổi tối khi bạn nhớ về ai đó mà bạn không còn gặp nữa. Trong khi chúng tôi đang đợi ở cùng cái đèn đỏ lần thứ ba, người tài xế ngân nga theo và gõ tay vào vô lăng bằng hai ngón. Khi cuối cùng chúng tôi đến khách sạn, tôi hỏi anh tên bài hát. Anh viết nó trên một mẩu giấy xé từ một cuốn sổ. Tôi vẫn nghe nó vào những buổi tối yên tĩnh, ngay cả bây giờ, bốn năm sau. Bài hát in vào đầu tôi, và con chim gỗ nhỏ cũng vậy.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct past simple sentence.
options: I take a black-and-yellow taxi from a small bookshop back to my hotel.
I have taken a black-and-yellow taxi from a small bookshop back to my hotel.
I took a black-and-yellow taxi from a small bookshop back to my hotel.
I was taking a black-and-yellow taxi from a small bookshop back to my hotel.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past simple 'took' for a finished past action.

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the correct 'while' + past continuous sentence.
options: While we waited at the same red light, the driver hummed along.
While we are waiting at the same red light, the driver hummed along.
While we wait at the same red light, the driver was hum along.
While we were waiting at the same red light, the driver hummed along.
unable to move because of heavy traffic
lost in a strange city
broken down on the road
driving very fast

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the natural sentence with 'turn up the radio'.
options: He turned the radio up high louder.
He turned up the radio.
He turn the up radio.
He has turn up the radio.
to shout the lyrics loudly
to dance with someone
to sing softly with closed mouth following the song
to write down the lyrics

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct preposition: 'a small wooden bird ___ his dashboard.'
options: in
by
at
on
mcQuestions.explanation: On a flat surface = 'on the dashboard'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct meaning of 'stuck in my head'.
options: I cannot stop thinking about it
I forgot it
I wrote it down
I sang it loudly
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Stuck in my head' = unable to forget (often about a song).

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the correct sentence with 'because of'.
options: We got stuck in heavy traffic because of it shower early.
We got stuck in heavy traffic because of an early monsoon shower.
We got stuck in heavy traffic because of was raining early.
We got stuck in heavy traffic because of an early monsoon shower was.
to learn for the first time
to forget completely
to know something or someone because you have seen, heard, or felt it before
to translate from another language

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the most natural sentence with 'a piece of paper torn from a notebook'.
options: He wrote it on a piece of paper torned from a notebook.
He wrote it on a piece of paper tear from a notebook.
He wrote it on a piece of paper tearing from a notebook.
He wrote it on a piece of paper torn from a notebook.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past participle 'torn' (from 'tear') as a reduced relative clause.

options: I still listen to it on quiet evenings, even now, four years later.
I still listening to it on quiet evenings, even now, four years later.
I still listen it on quiet evenings, even now, four years later.
I am still listen to it on quiet evenings, even now, four years later.
mcQuestions.explanation: Present simple 'listen to' fits the still-ongoing habit.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Why do some songs we hear by accident, in a taxi or on a bus, stay with us longer than songs we choose carefully ourselves?
